sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what happened after clicking on the M square
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"=== M squares ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M':
        print(f"  {n['boundary']}")

# Check white bar
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
white_count = ascii_lines[63].count('W')
print(f"\nWhite bar count: {white_count}")
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The M square at (36-37, 17-18) moved to (35-36, 16-17). It moved up by 1 and left by 1. The white bar went from 46 to 44.
So clicking on an M square moves it diagonally up-left by 1 cell!
This is similar to level 1, where clicking on the gray square moved it in a spiral pattern. But in level 2, clicking on an M square moves it diagonally up-left.
The goal is likely to move all 4 M squares to fill the yellow 4x4 square at (3-6, 30-33).
Let me figure out how many clicks it takes to move each M square to the yellow square.
Current M square positions:
- (35-36, 16-17) - need to move to yellow square
- (39-40, 36-37) - need to move to yellow square
- (55-56, 46-47) - need to move to yellow square
- (56-57, 15-16) - need to move to yellow square
The yellow square is at (3-6, 30-33). To fill it with 4 M squares (2x2 each), they need to be at:
- (3-4, 30-31) - top-left of yellow square
- (3-4, 32-33) - top-right of yellow square
- (5-6, 30-31) - bottom-left of yellow square
- (5-6, 32-33) - bottom-right of yellow square
Each click on an M square moves it up-left by 1. So to move an M square from (r, c) to (r', c'), I need (r - r') clicks (assuming r > r' and c > c').
But wait, the M squares move diagonally up-left, so each click decreases both row and column by 1. So the number of clicks needed is max(r - r', c - c') if the target is up-left of the current position.
But the M squares are below and to the left/right of the yellow square. Let me check:
- (35-36, 16-17): row 35-36, col 16-17. Yellow square is at row 3-6, col 30-33. The M square is below and to the left of the yellow square. To move it to the yellow square, I need to move it up and right. But clicking moves it up-left, not up-right. So this M square can't reach the yellow square by clicking on it.
Wait, maybe the M squares move in a different direction depending on their position relative to the yellow square. Or maybe clicking on the blue shape changes the direction.
Let me re-examine. In level 1, clicking on the blue octagon moved the gray square in a spiral pattern. In level 2, clicking on the blue shape doesn't move the M squares, but clicking on an M square moves it diagonally up-left.
Maybe the goal is not to move the M squares to the yellow square. Maybe the goal is to move the M squares to specific positions, and the yellow square is just a marker.
Or maybe the M squares move in a spiral pattern when clicked, like in level 1. Let me click on the same M square again to see if it continues moving in a pattern.
